$comment: External assets that are NOT committed to the repository.
Anything here is either too large to live in git history (a mesh in the hundreds of MB is
irreversible once committed) or carries a licence that requires the user to fetch it
themselves rather than have it redistributed. Fetch with:
Every consumer (scene, test) must degrade gracefully when an asset is absent: SKIP with a
message naming the fetch command, never fail. A missing optional asset is a setup step the
user has not run, not a broken build.
Fields: name, url, sha256 (of the DOWNLOADED file, before any unpacking), dest (repo-relative
path of the FINAL file), unpack (none|gzip), sizeBytes, triangles, licence, credit, tags.

assets.name: xyzrgb-dragon
assets.description: Stanford XYZ RGB Asian Dragon — 7.2M triangles. The geometry stress asset for the virtualized-geometry (Nanite) pipeline: dense enough that the classic renderer chokes and the far-field triangles go genuinely sub-pixel, which is the regime cluster LOD exists for.
assets.licence: Free to use; the Stanford Computer Graphics Laboratory asks that the source be acknowledged.
assets.credit: Stanford Computer Graphics Laboratory, 3D Scanning Repository (https://graphics.stanford.edu/data/3Dscanrep/)
